description_long: This is a classic biplane with a striking red fuselage and wings, featuring roundels on the upper wing surfaces and a tricolor rudder at the tail. The aircraft has two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires, giving it a vintage, early-20th-century appearance. The nose houses a single propeller engine, and the cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, likely open and designed for one or two occupants. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, typical of taildragger configurations. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted red with accent markings. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable airplane suitable for display, training, or recreational flying.
description: A vintage red biplane with dual wings, a single propeller engine, and fixed taildragger landing gear. It features roundels on the wings and a tricolor rudder, evoking early aviation style.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red fuselage, single propeller, and tricolor rudder, with the upper wing's roundel visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the dual biplane wings, both with roundels, and the full span of the tail and landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the propeller, cockpit placement, and tail details, with the tricolor rudder prominent.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the propeller, open cockpit, and the symmetrical arrangement of the biplane wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single or tandem seat.
